patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: "Those who try to carry out a one-sided coup... should know that we are not committed to them in any way"
patterns.explanation: Frames opponents as attempting a 'one-sided coup', creating an us/them dynamic.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: "Not only will we fight... but I’m also making it clear that we’ll cancel them on the day we return to power"
patterns.explanation: Presents a false choice between fighting reforms and returning to power, hiding complexity.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: we are all standing together.
patterns.explanation: Framing opposition support as a binary choice, ignoring potential for nuanced criticism.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: The entire nation of Israel stands behind you until the destruction of the Iranian threat.
patterns.explanation: Portrays Israeli unity against a threatening out-group (Iran) without nuance.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: I don’t want to save Netanyahu, but to save the hostages.
patterns.explanation: Framing the situation as a conflict between saving hostages and political ally Netanyahu.

patterns.name: emotional_manipulation
patterns.quote: In Hamas’s tunnels, there are 50 hostages… Every hostage whose life is at risk could be our son, your son.
patterns.explanation: Using emotional appeal to Holocaust imagery and familial ties to underscore urgency.

patterns.name: whataboutism
patterns.quote: Making it clear he believed Netanyahu was currently avoiding a Gaza deal due to political pressure from his far-right allies rather than valid security considerations.
patterns.explanation: Deflecting criticism of Gantz's own political motivations by attributing similar motives to Netanyahu.

violations.title: Gantz warns Israel is 'governed by extremists who have no endgame and no accountability'

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: Ben-Gvir and Smotrich do not want victory — they want chaos
patterns.explanation: Framing the government as chaotic, implying a virtuous opposition.

patterns.name: group_discrediting
patterns.quote: The professionals gave the politicians a path — the politicians chose their coalition over their country.
patterns.explanation: Discredits political leaders by contrasting them with military professionals.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: Gantz stopped short of calling for immediate elections, saying the priority must be securing the hostages and establishing a credible day-after plan before any political transition.
patterns.explanation: Implies that only two options exist: securing hostages or holding elections.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: Torah scholars would be called criminals?
patterns.explanation: Deri frames Torah scholars as victims, implying a moral divide between them and others.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: if it's not regulated, we'll go to elections.
patterns.explanation: Deri presents a false choice between passing the law and going to elections, ignoring other potential solutions.

violations.title: Deri: 'If the Torah world is drafted, the state loses its soul — we will bring this government down'

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: This is a secular crusade against our way of life.
patterns.explanation: Framing the conscription bill as an existential threat to Jewish identity.

patterns.name: dehumanization
patterns.quote: They want to send yeshiva students to the front not because they need soldiers but because they want to destroy Torah Judaism.
patterns.explanation: Portraying secularists as intent on destroying a way of life, potentially justifying extreme responses.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: This is not about military service. This is about whether a Jewish state has room for Jews who live as Jews have lived for three thousand years.
patterns.explanation: Dividing society into religious and secular factions, presenting them in conflict.

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: We will bring this government down
patterns.explanation: Threatening to destabilize the government if their demands are not met, creating fear.

patterns.name: group_discrediting
patterns.quote: reckless deal will lead to release of hundreds of murderous terrorists
patterns.explanation: Ben Gvir attacks the credibility of the hostage deal by labeling freed prisoners as 'murderous terrorists', ignoring context.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: When we see the jubilant cheers of Hamas supporter [Hadash-Ta'al party leader] Ayman Odeh, the dancing in Gaza
patterns.explanation: Ben Gvir frames supporters of the deal as aligned with 'Hamas', implying they are enemies of Israel without naming them.

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: existing deal increases Hamas’s appetite and motivation to carry out another October 7 massacre
patterns.explanation: Ben Gvir exaggerates the threat posed by the deal, invoking fear of a repeat attack without evidence.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: What I want, and this is also what Netanyahu promised me, is the dismantlement of Hamas
patterns.explanation: Framing Hamas as an existential threat requiring total destruction.

patterns.name: call_to_violence
patterns.quote: if he doesn’t dismantle Hamas, he knows very well what will happen
patterns.explanation: Implicit call for violent action against Hamas if demands are not met.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: Ben Gvir declined to say how much time he had given Netanyahu to comply with his demands
patterns.explanation: Creates a false choice between compliance and unspecified consequences.

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: he knows very well what will happen
patterns.explanation: Uses vague threats to provoke fear without specifying the consequences.

violations.title: Ben-Gvir: 'Gaza must be emptied — those who stay are choosing to be our enemies'

patterns.name: call_to_violence
patterns.quote: 'Gaza must be emptied — those who stay are choosing to be our enemies'
patterns.explanation: Explicit call for population transfer, framing remaining civilians as enemies.

patterns.name: dehumanization
patterns.quote: 'there is no such thing as an innocent bystander in a society that produces human animals'
patterns.explanation: Dehumanizes Gaza population, justifying extreme measures.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: 'anyone who provides shelter, food, or moral support to those who seek our destruction'
patterns.explanation: Frames opponents as existential threat, justifying extreme measures.

patterns.name: demonization
patterns.quote: 'enemies in robes' 'should be removed from the bench and investigated for aiding terror'
patterns.explanation: Dramatically demonizes Supreme Court justices, justifying removal.

patterns.name: scapegoating
patterns.quote: 'The world screams when we defend ourselves. The world was silent when they slaughtered us.'
patterns.explanation: Redirects blame to the international community, avoiding internal accountability.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: order the IDF to immediately prepare for the full occupation of the Gaza Strip and the central camps
patterns.explanation: Framing Hamas as an existential threat requiring total territorial control.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: Instead of handing over territory to the enemy, Israel must take territory from the enemy
patterns.explanation: Simplifies complex geopolitical issues into a binary us/them conflict.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: Without settlements, there will be no security
patterns.explanation: Presents territorial expansion as the only path to security, ignoring diplomatic alternatives.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: territory relinquished by Israel in the past had turned into 'Iranian forward terror bases', and endangered the country
patterns.explanation: Frames loss of territory as existential threat, justifying preemptive actions.

patterns.name: dehumanization
patterns.quote: We can occupy Gaza and thin the population by half within two years
patterns.explanation: Dehumanizes Gazan Palestinians by reducing them to a 'population' needing 'thinning'.

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: If the cost of security control is 5 billion shekels ($1.37 billion), I will accept it with open arms
patterns.explanation: Exaggerates threat posed by Gaza to provoke fear and acceptance of high costs.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: Occupying Gaza is not a dirty word
patterns.explanation: Frames occupation as virtuous, implicitly dehumanizing and demonizing Gazan Palestinians.

patterns.name: whataboutism
patterns.quote: If the cost of security control is 5 billion shekels ($1.37 billion), I will accept it with open arms
patterns.explanation: Deflects criticism by suggesting financial concerns are irrelevant compared to 'security'.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: Smotrich implied blocking aid to Gaza is 'justified and moral' if it causes two million civilians to die of hunger.
patterns.explanation: Framing the starvation of 2 million civilians as a justified response, creating justification for extreme actions.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: Smotrich claims aid is plundered by Hamas, extending the war.
patterns.explanation: Blames Hamas for prolonging the conflict while downplaying Israel's role in humanitarian blockades.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: Smotrich asserts that aid is brought 'because there is no choice'.
patterns.explanation: Presents humanitarian aid as a binary choice, ignoring other potential solutions or moral considerations.

violations.title: Smotrich outlines 'voluntary emigration' plan: 'Two million can leave within 18 months if we create the right conditions'

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: International law was written by people who have never faced genocide themselves.
patterns.explanation: Framing international law as authored by those who 'never faced genocide' implies justification for preemptive actions.

patterns.name: dehumanization
patterns.quote: If we remove all incentives to stay... two million people can be relocated within 18 months.
patterns.explanation: Describes mass relocation as a 'rational choice', dehumanizing those affected by stripping their agency.

patterns.name: scapegoating
patterns.quote: humanitarian aid was 'subsidizing hostility'
patterns.explanation: Blames humanitarian aid for conflict, avoiding systemic analysis of root causes.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: countries that refuse to absorb Gaza's population are 'hypocrites who cry for Palestinians but won't give a single one a visa'.
patterns.explanation: Framing other countries as hypocritical deflects responsibility and creates an us/them dichotomy.

patterns.name: whataboutism
patterns.quote: UN warnings about mass starvation are 'calculated political pressure from organizations that have never accepted Israel's right to exist in security.'
patterns.explanation: Deflects criticism by questioning the motives of UN and other organizations.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: I hope and believe the committee will be formed and will prevent the destruction of our democracy
patterns.explanation: Frames judicial reform as a threat to democracy, creating a false choice between current system and 'destruction'.

patterns.name: emotional_manipulation
patterns.quote: prevent the destruction of our democracy and the terrible division among the people of Israel
patterns.explanation: Uses emotionally charged language ('destruction', 'terrible division') to sway public opinion without evidence.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: We are all united on the operation
patterns.explanation: Framing opposition as unified behind government, creating 'us vs them' dynamic.

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: We are united behind the pilots, fighters and security forces in the most justified operation there is
patterns.explanation: Appeals to fear of external threat (Iran) to justify military action.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: This is not a unity government. This is not an emergency government.
patterns.explanation: Framing the current government as illegitimate and harmful, creating 'us vs them' dynamic.

patterns.name: whataboutism
patterns.quote: For weeks now, the poison machine of Netanyahu and his aides has been attacking the chief of staff, the army, the commanders and the fighters.
patterns.explanation: Redirecting criticism by accusing opponents of undermining military leadership.

patterns.name: group_discrediting
patterns.quote: as long as they are there, as long as they sit under Netanyahu, they give [his actions] legitimacy.
patterns.explanation: Attacking the credibility of coalition members by associating them with Netanyahu.

patterns.name: demonization
patterns.quote: Hamas will not rule again in Gaza.
patterns.explanation: Framing Hamas as an absolute evil with no future, inciting further action against them.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: He carried out the worst massacre in our people's history since the Holocaust.
patterns.explanation: Accusing Hamas of existential threat by comparing them to historical genocides.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: Today, evil suffered a heavy blow, but our mission is not yet completed.
patterns.explanation: Framing the conflict as a battle between absolute good (Israel) and absolute evil (Hamas).

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: Those who harm our hostages, his blood is on his head.
patterns.explanation: Using fear to pressure hostage-takers and justify potential violent responses.

patterns.name: emotional_manipulation
patterns.quote: Today, we showed the world the victory of the good over the evil.
patterns.explanation: Using emotionally charged language to frame Israel's actions as morally superior.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: crisis of faith... reached our enemies — who took pleasure in this and derived significant benefit from it.
patterns.explanation: Framing the conflict as a battle between Israel and unspecified 'enemies', creating an us/them dynamic.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: this situation cannot continue.
patterns.explanation: Presenting the firing of Gallant as the only option, ignoring other potential solutions to the conflict.

patterns.name: demonization
patterns.quote: savage enemies who seek our annihilation
patterns.explanation: Framing opponents as absolute evil with no redeeming qualities, justifying defensive actions.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: destroy our common civilization and return all of us to a dark age of tyranny and terror
patterns.explanation: Portraying enemies as planning civilizational destruction, justifying preemptive action.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: the curse of Iran’s unremitting aggression or the blessing of a historic reconciliation between Arab and Jew
patterns.explanation: Framing events as conflict between virtuous in-group (Israel) and threatening out-group.

patterns.name: emotional_manipulation
patterns.quote: They burned babies alive. They burned entire families alive—babies, children, parents, grandparents
patterns.explanation: Using emotionally charged language to drive conclusions without providing factual evidence.

patterns.name: dehumanization
patterns.quote: clash between barbarism and civilization
patterns.explanation: Framing opponents as 'barbaric' dehumanizes them, making violence seem justified.

patterns.name: demonization
patterns.quote: forces of civilization to triumph... America and Israel must stand together
patterns.explanation: Portrays opponents as absolute evil, justifying alliance between US and Israel.

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: day that will forever live in infamy... like 20 9/11s in one day
patterns.explanation: Exaggerates threat to justify extreme measures and unity.

patterns.name: emotional_manipulation
patterns.quote: dragged 255 people into the dark dungeons of Gaza
patterns.explanation: Uses emotionally charged language to provoke sympathy and anger.

violations.title: Netanyahu: 'The axis of evil is crumbling — history will judge those who doubted us'

patterns.name: existential_threat_accusation
patterns.quote: Everything I warned about for thirty years is now proven true.
patterns.explanation: Frames political opponents as existential threats to Israel.

patterns.name: false_dilemma
patterns.quote: Either you stand with Israel or you stand with those who want to destroy it.
patterns.explanation: Presents a false binary choice, ignoring nuanced positions.

patterns.name: whataboutism
patterns.quote: They said we were paranoid. Now the axis of evil is crumbling before our eyes.
patterns.explanation: Redirects criticism by pointing to supposed failures of opponents.

patterns.name: appeal_to_fear
patterns.quote: There is no middle ground when your house is on fire.
patterns.explanation: Exaggerates threat to provoke fear and demand immediate action.

patterns.name: us_vs_them
patterns.quote: They stabbed us in the back when we needed them most.
patterns.explanation: Framing events as a conflict between virtuous Israel and traitorous opponents.
